title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXXXVI. Lullabies.
content:
[From 'The Pleasant Com[oe]die of Patient Grissell,' 1603.]

        Hush, hush, hush, hush!
        And I dance mine own child,
        And I dance mine own child,
        Hush, hush, hush, hush!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXXXVI. Lullabies.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
